meta.title: Vertical transmission of the Piry rhabdovirus by sigma virus-infected Drosophila melanogaster females -- Ohanessian 70 (1): 209 -- Journal of General Virology

abstractHtml: Piry rhabdovirus is not transmitted from Drosophila melanogaster females to their progeny. However, in mixed infections with sigma, another rhabdovirus, bearing the g+ genetic marker, Piry may occasionally be transmitted to offspring. Thus, an endemic Drosophila virus can act as a helper virus enabling vertical transmission of a virus pathogenic to vertebrates.
